title: Responsive layout for public documentation views
description: Ensure the public layout (navigation, content, search, landing page) is fully usable on mobile and tablet viewports, per the PRD's explicit responsiveness requirement for the public-facing site.
acceptanceCriteria: On narrow viewports (< 768px), the sidebar navigation collapses behind a toggle (hamburger menu) rather than being permanently visible and squeezing content
Content area (prose, tables, code blocks, component-embed iframes) reflows without horizontal scrolling of the page itself; wide elements (tables, code) scroll within their own container instead
Search UI (TASK-54) remains usable on mobile (e.g. as a full-width overlay rather than a small dropdown)
Touch targets (nav items, toggle buttons) meet a reasonable minimum tap size on mobile
Verify in browser using playwright at both a desktop and a mobile viewport size

steps.description: Add responsive breakpoints to the layout shell
steps.details: Update `apps/admin/src/features/public/PublicLayout.tsx` (from TASK-52) with a CSS breakpoint (e.g. 768px) toggling between a permanent sidebar (desktop) and a collapsible off-canvas nav (mobile).
Add a hamburger button visible only below the breakpoint, opening/closing the sidebar as an overlay with a backdrop.

steps.description: Constrain wide content to scroll within its container
steps.details: Apply `overflow-x: auto` wrappers around tables and code blocks in `PageRenderer` (TASK-50) styling, and set `max-width: 100%` on iframes/images so nothing forces the page body to scroll horizontally.
On narrow viewports, render the search trigger as an icon that opens a full-width/full-height search overlay instead of a small inline dropdown.

steps.description: Verify in browser at multiple viewports
steps.details: Using Playwright, set viewport to a common desktop size (e.g. 1440x900) and a common mobile size (e.g. 390x844); confirm nav toggle behavior, absence of page-level horizontal scroll on a page containing a wide table, and that search remains usable at both sizes.

technicalNotes: This task only covers the public-facing site per PRD scope ('Responsividade da visualização pública') — the admin editing panel is not required to be mobile-responsive for the MVP.
